expectations.text: Output is exactly one self-contained .html file — no separate CSS/JS/asset files are required to open it
expectations.evidence: Exactly one .html file (invoicing-dashboard.html, 30,834 bytes). All CSS is in an inline <style> block (lines 7-196) and all JS in an inline <script> (lines 343-652). grep for '<script src=', '<link href=', 'url(', and '<img' found zero external references, so the file opens standalone. Extra non-required artifacts present in outputs/: screenshot-1-dashboard.png, screenshot-2-marked-paid.png, screenshot-3-new-invoice-form.png, screenshot-4-final.png (allowed per grading rule, do not fail the assertion).

expectations.text: No network data calls: the file contains no fetch()/XMLHttpRequest/axios usage (CDN <script>/<link> tags are allowed)
expectations.evidence: grep -nE 'fetch\(|XMLHttpRequest|axios' returns no matches. There are also no CDN <script>/<link> tags at all. Persistence is localStorage only (lines 376-383), which is not a network call.

expectations.text: Demo data is hardcoded in the file, and interactions mutate it in memory so the UI updates without a page reload
expectations.evidence: seed() (lines 354-369) hardcodes 14 invoices with ids INV-2026-001..014. All interactions mutate the in-memory `invoices` array and re-render without reload: 'pay' sets inv.status='paid' then render() (lines 549-554, 571); 'del' filters the array (line 567); form submit does invoices.unshift(inv) then render() (lines 623-627). render() (lines 418-423) redraws stats, table, and chart via DOM updates only — no location.reload or form navigation (form submit calls e.preventDefault(), line 606).

expectations.text: The invoice list shows a status per invoice (e.g. draft/sent/paid/overdue)
expectations.evidence: Each table row renders a status pill: `${stLabel}` (line 467) with stLabel mapped from {paid, sent, overdue, draft} (line 457). effectiveStatus() (lines 392-395) derives 'overdue' for sent invoices past due. Seed data covers all four statuses: 1 draft, sent, overdue (e.g. INV-2026-011 due May 22 vs TODAY), and 9 paid.

expectations.text: The create-invoice flow adds a new invoice to the visible list in memory
expectations.evidence: Click path traced: '+ New Invoice' button (id=newInvoiceBtn, line 211) → openModal() (line 599) opens the form modal; submit handler (lines 605-628) builds an invoice object with nextId() and does `invoices.unshift(inv)` (line 623) followed by render(), so the new row appears at the top of the table (default filter 'all'). flashId highlights the new row (line 624, animation lines 106-107). No persistence layer required — pure in-memory array mutation.

expectations.text: Marking an invoice paid changes its status and the change is reflected in the UI (totals and/or chart update)
expectations.evidence: 'Mark paid' buttons render for unpaid rows (lines 471-472, data-act='pay'). The handler (lines 549-554) sets inv.status='paid' and inv.paidOn=today, then render() (line 571) calls renderStats() — recomputing Outstanding/Overdue/Paid-this-month/YTD from the invoices array (lines 425-438) — and renderChart(), which adds the amount to the paid month's collected bar (lines 491-499). A confirmation toast also fires (line 553). Screenshot-2-marked-paid.png corroborates.

expectations.text: A monthly revenue chart renders from the hardcoded data
expectations.evidence: renderChart() (lines 487-539) builds an inline SVG stacked bar chart into #chart (svg element, line 277) directly from the `invoices` array: collected revenue bucketed by paidOn month, billed-unpaid by issued month (lines 489-499), with gridlines, month labels, and a 'Collected so far' total (line 538). It is called on initial load via render() (line 650), so it renders from the hardcoded seed data.

expectations.text: An empty state (a list/screen with no data) is reachable by clicking
expectations.evidence:
Empty state exists: `
No invoices match this filter.
` (line 266), shown when the filtered row set is empty (line 450: emptyState.style.display = rows.length ? 'none' : 'block'). Traced click path: the seed has exactly one draft (INV-2026-014, line 355); click its 'Send' button (rendered only for drafts, line 470; handler lines 561-564 sets status='sent'), then click the 'Drafts' filter chip (line 251, handler lines 575-581) → renderTable filters to 0 rows → empty state displays. Alternative path: every row has a delete button (line 474, handler lines 566-568), so clicking delete on all rows empties the entire list.

expectations.text: A loading state (spinner or skeleton) is reachable, e.g. simulated with setTimeout
expectations.evidence: No loading state exists anywhere in the file. grep -niE 'spinner|skeleton|loading' returns zero matches; the only setTimeout is the toast auto-hide timer (line 646: `toastTimer = setTimeout(() => el.classList.remove("show"), 3200)`). All actions (mark paid, create, delete, filter) apply synchronously with no simulated latency, spinner, or skeleton UI.

expectations.text: An error message or toast is reachable by clicking
expectations.evidence: Two traced click paths. Toast: clicking 'Mark paid' calls toast(`${inv.id} marked paid — ...`) (line 553); toast element at line 341, show logic lines 641-647 — every action button (pay/unpay/send/del/create/reset) fires one. Error message: the form has novalidate (line 299), so clicking 'Create invoice' with blank fields runs the submit handler's validation (lines 614-617), which displays #formError: 'Please fill in client, project, a positive amount and both dates.' (line 332).

expectations.text: An overflow case (too-long text or oversized value) is present so the user can see how the layout copes
expectations.evidence: No overflow case is present in the seeded data. All 14 seed invoices (lines 355-368) have short client names (longest: 'Bluefin Analytics', 17 chars) and short project strings (longest: 'App icon + design system audit', ~30 chars); amounts range $1,600-$6,100 — nothing oversized. There is also no truncation/ellipsis CSS for cell content (grep for 'ellipsis|truncat|break-word|word-break' finds nothing; the only overflow rules are generic card overflow:hidden at line 77, sr-only at line 101, and modal scroll at line 170). A user could type long text into the create form, but no seeded variant demonstrates how the layout copes.

execution_metrics.notes: No metrics.json was produced by the executor; output_chars counts invoicing-dashboard.html only (the 4 PNG screenshots are binary).

claims.claim: The in-page tip says clicking 'Mark paid' updates the chart and stat cards
claims.evidence: The 'pay' handler mutates status/paidOn and render() recomputes renderStats() and renderChart() (lines 549-554, 418-423), so the tip's claim holds.

claims.claim: The tip says 'Changes are saved locally' and 'Reset demo' restores the sample data
claims.evidence: save() persists to localStorage on every render (lines 383, 422); resetBtn handler reseeds invoices and re-renders (lines 631-637).

claims.claim: Screenshots depict the working dashboard states (initial, marked-paid, new-invoice form, final)
claims.evidence: Four PNGs exist in outputs/ matching those names; their states correspond to code paths verified above (no transcript was provided to cross-check the capture process).

suggestions.assertion: An empty state (a list/screen with no data) is reachable by clicking
suggestions.reason: This passes via an incidental path (change the lone draft's status, then click the Drafts filter; or delete all rows) rather than a deliberately designed empty state. If the intent is a designed empty state, consider tightening the wording, e.g. 'an intentional empty-state view with messaging, reachable in <=2 clicks'.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are discriminating — the two failures (no loading state, no seeded overflow case) are exactly the robustness states a generic build skips, which is what this eval should detect. The localStorage persistence in this output did not conflict with the 'in memory' assertion, but a stricter variant could specify whether persistence is allowed.
